name: INJI PULI
intro: Eat it with the gravies from the book and some fluffy rice or with rotis—either way, this Kerala-style side that falls somewhere between a chutney and a pickle is magical.
method: In a pan, heat the oil and add the mustard seeds. Once they begin to crackle, add the dried red chillies, curry leaves, asafoetida and turmeric powder and stir well.
Add the ginger and green chillies and sauté them for 2-3 minutes on low flame.
Add the tamarind paste and stir well.
Meanwhile, in another pan, melt the jaggery and add it to the tempered tamarind. Adjust the salt and allow the chutney to cool to room temperature.
